chosen_logo.why: Inline SVG in the header with viewBox 0 0 60 25 and a single path — the canonical Stripe wordmark, set in Stripe's own letterforms. 60×25 is the recognizable wordmark aspect ratio. JSON-LD candidate (455ac10f) is on Stripeassets CDN as a fallback if the inline SVG quality-gates fail.

brand_primary.name: --color-brand-slate
brand_primary.evidence: Stripe's signature slate navy — the dominant chord across the brand site. Literal hex appears 426× in CSS; used as cover backgrounds, dark-mode surfaces, and headline body text on subtle pages. Computed body color rgb(10,37,64) confirms this as the canonical slate.

brand_secondary.name: --color-brand-indigo
brand_secondary.evidence: Stripe's iconic indigo/violet — the accent/CTA color. Literal hex appears 128× in CSS. The 'Stripe purple' that anchors brand campaigns, button fills, and decorative gradients. Together with --slate, the two-chord chord that defines Stripe's visual language.

ink.name: --color-brand-ink
ink.evidence: Computed body color across pages: rgb(66,84,102). Stripe avoids pure black — uses warm-cool slate gray for body text. Distinctive 'engineered calm' typography colour.

paper.name: --color-brand-paper
paper.evidence: Pure white surface for content-dense pages (pricing, customers). Slate-bg sections invert: white type on slate. Two-mode design — slate-navy hero OR white surface, with the brand indigo as the consistent thread.

accents.name: --color-brand-lavender
accents.evidence: Lavender — the softer companion to the iconic indigo. 134× in CSS. Used for decorative gradients and section markers.

accents.name: --color-brand-blue
accents.evidence: Stripe's true blue — used for inline links and informational accents. 123× in CSS.

accents.name: --color-brand-indigo-deep
accents.evidence: Darker indigo — used for heading-body type and emphasized text on white surfaces. 119× in CSS.

accents.name: --color-brand-electric
accents.evidence: Vivid blue-violet — emphasis accent within the indigo family. 90× in CSS.

accents.name: --color-brand-mist
accents.evidence: Pale paper-tint for subtle row separation and product-surface backgrounds. 79× in CSS.

accents.name: --color-brand-fog
accents.evidence: Cool grey-blue divider colour. 122× in CSS.

neutrals.name: --color-brand-slate-mid
neutrals.evidence: Muted slate-indigo middle tone. 84× in CSS.

display_font.evidence: Computed h1/h2/body font on every page: 'sohne-var, "Helvetica Neue", Arial, sans-serif'. Söhne is Stripe's licensed proprietary face from Klim Type Foundry — the brand's most distinctive typographic choice. 75× in font frequencies; the brand commits to one face for everything.

body_font.evidence: Same as display. Stripe uses Söhne across nav, body, and titles; weight differentiates display vs body.

mono_font.evidence: Used 12× across the brand site for code samples — fits Stripe's developer-tooling identity.

spacing_radius_shadow.evidence: Computed h1, button, and a have radius:0px — Stripe commits to sharp rectangles across surfaces and CTAs. No box-shadow on top-level chrome; product surfaces use very subtle 1-2px shadows. Body padY is generous; the engineering-doc rhythm carries through every page.

precedents: Bloomberg Terminal — but warm and humanist
modernist Swiss editorial — but with engineered fintech confidence
aesthetic.voice_register: technical precision; developer-tone narrative ('Payments infrastructure for the internet', 'Atlas for incorporation'); the brand speaks the way an experienced engineer documents a system
